features.name: timescale_precision_non_unity_multiplier
features.description: Code should include a timescale directive with a non-1 precision multiplier (e.g., `10ps`, `100ps`, `5ns`) rather than only single-unit precision like `1ps` or `1ns`. Verilator's sc_set_time_resolution parsing has a limitation where multi-digit time unit values (10, 100) are misparsed as separate digits, causing mismatches between SystemC and Verilog time precision when using non-unity multipliers in timescale declarations.
